question: Will Mara Voss's scheduling copilot reach a committed public launch or at least three signed design partners by mid-September 2025?

person.description: A human actor in the simulation

scheduling_copilot.description: The software product being developed by Mara Voss's company

design_partner_agreement.description: A signed agreement with a design partner for the scheduling copilot

board_meeting.description: A formal meeting of the seed investor board

pilot_report.description: Results report from the Calder Logistics enterprise pilot

pilot_expansion_decision.description: Decision by Devon Reyes on whether to expand the Calder Logistics pilot

board_approval_record.description: Record of board approval or rejection of launch spend over $50k

launch_spend_execution.description: Actual spending on public launch activities

design_partner_count.description: Running count of signed design partners

founder_of.description: Person is founder/CEO of a company

runs_pilot_at.description: Person runs enterprise pilot at a company

lead_investor_of.description: Person is lead seed investor and chairs board

board_member_of.description: Person serves on board of company

signed_partner_with.description: Design partner agreement between company and partner

pilot_results_submitted.description: Devon Reyes submits pilot results report

pilot_expansion_approved.description: Devon Reyes decides to expand pilot

board_vote_cast.description: A board member casts vote on launch spend

design_partner_signed.description: A new design partner agreement is signed

public_launch_committed.description: Mara Voss commits to a public launch date

launch_spend_authorized.description: Spending for launch activities is authorized

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

pilot_evaluation_process.description: Process by which Devon Reyes evaluates and decides on pilot expansion

board_approval_process.description: Process for board approval of launch spend over $50k

seed_investor_board.description: 3-seat board that must approve any launch spend over $50k
seed_investor_board.evidence: Known: Priya Shah chairs 3-seat board. Mara Voss is founder/CEO and likely holds a seat. Third member unknown but assumed.

launch_spend_budget.description: Mara Voss has $40k discretionary launch budget without board approval
launch_spend_budget.rule: spend_amount <= 40000 implies no board approval needed; spend_amount > 40000 requires board_approval_record.approval_status == 'approved'

pilot_timeline.description: Pilot results expected in ~3 weeks from prediction time (2025-07-18)

horizon_deadline.rule: All events must occur on or before 2025-09-16

discretionary_launch_budget.description: $40k available for launch activities without board approval

pilot_infrastructure.description: Calder Logistics pilot infrastructure and data

board_approval_visibility.description: Board approval records are visible to all board members and Mara Voss

pilot_results_visibility.description: Pilot results are visible to Devon Reyes and Mara Voss initially

design_partner_count_visibility.description: Signed design partner count is public information

mara_voss.why_consequential: Decides whether to commit to public launch, manages design partner acquisition, controls discretionary budget up to $40k
affordances: negotiate and sign design partner agreements
spend up to $40k on launch activities without board approval

devon_reyes.why_consequential: Controls pilot expansion decision which may influence launch timing and partner interest
affordances: decide to expand or terminate pilot

priya_shah.why_consequential: Chairs board that must approve any launch spend over $50k; can influence other board members
affordances: cast vote on launch spend proposals

unresolved_mechanisms: Identity and voting behavior of the third board member is unknown; assumed to exist but no evidence of who they are or how they vote.
Whether Mara Voss will propose launch spend exceeding $40k (triggering board approval) or keep spend under that threshold is unresolved.
Whether Devon Reyes will recommend pilot expansion or not is unresolved; depends on pilot results not yet submitted.
Number of design partners currently in negotiation pipeline is unknown; only known that zero are signed as of prediction time.
assumptions: The third board member exists and will participate in any vote on launch spend over $50k.
Pilot results will be submitted by approximately 2025-08-08 (3 weeks from prediction time).
A 'committed public launch' means a publicly announced launch date, not necessarily that the launch has occurred.
Design partners must be signed (contract executed) by the horizon date to count toward the threshold.

auto_repairs: institution 'seed_investor_board' aggregation defaulted to majority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No entity or fact type for the third board member's identity, voting preference, or relationship to Mara Voss
No entity or fact type for the Calder Logistics pilot's actual performance metrics (e.g., user satisfaction, error rate, time saved) that would drive Devon Reyes's decision
No entity or fact type for Mara Voss's personal financial runway or burn rate, which constrains whether she can afford launch spend without board approval
No entity or fact type for competitive landscape or market timing pressure that might force a premature launch
No entity or fact type for the design partner pipeline quality (e.g., how many are in negotiation, what stage, likelihood to convert)
relabeled_progress_bars: The 'design_partner_acquisition_process' has only three states (prospecting, negotiation, agreement_signed) — no state for 'due_diligence', 'legal_review', or 'contract_execution_delayed', which are common real-world blockers
The 'board_approval_process' lacks a state for 'proposal_withdrawn' or 'board_adjourned_without_vote', which could stall indefinitely
The 'pilot_evaluation_process' has no state for 'results_inconclusive' or 'decision_deferred', which would leave the process stuck
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes the third board member exists and votes, but if that seat is vacant or the member is a passive investor, the board may never reach a quorum or majority

The model treats 'design_partner_signed' as a binary event, but a signed agreement may include escape clauses, non-binding MOUs, or conditional terms that make 'signed' not equivalent to 'committed partner'

The model does not capture that Mara Voss might unilaterally commit to a public launch date (public_launch_committed) even without board approval, creating a de facto launch that the model would count as success but which could be financially catastrophic
direct_human_reaction_encodings: No encoding of Priya Shah's personal risk tolerance or past voting patterns on spend proposals
No encoding of Devon Reyes's relationship with Mara Voss (e.g., trust, skepticism) that could bias the pilot evaluation
No encoding of the design partners' own internal approval timelines or budget cycles, which often delay signing
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: The model treats 'public_launch_committed' as a semantic event, but a public commitment could be a marketing stunt with no actual product readiness — the model does not distinguish between a real launch and a vaporware announcement
The model treats 'design_partner_signed' as a fact, but a design partner may be a friendly company that signs without real intent to use the product, inflating the count
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No representation of software development velocity, bug counts, or feature completion that would determine if the copilot is actually launchable
No representation of external dependencies (e.g., API integrations, cloud infrastructure, regulatory compliance) that could block launch
No representation of calendar time — the model has no mechanism to enforce that mid-September 2025 is a deadline, so events could occur after the deadline without penalty
